tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent holds at $100.84/bbl on Mar 13 as Iran's new Supreme Leader Khamenei explicitly vowed Hormuz remains blocked — eliminating the residual near-term normalisation case. The Mar 11 reserve release softened tail risk but did not restore shipping confidence; six more vessels were struck on Mar 12. Hold core long exposure; the working range is $98-118 and stress case remains $120+. Pezeshkian's ceasefire feeler is a conditional signal only — no material probability shift until terms are accepted. PTTEP and Medco remain the cleanest ASEAN equity beta.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent is at $100.84 on Mar 13 with Khamenei's Hormuz commitment removing any near-term relief path. ASEAN Energy Equities proxy is 146.1 (+0.7%), while ASEAN Banks proxy is flat at 198.7 as inflation and subsidy risk remains priced in. Rebuild energy exposure on producers and integrated names; fade rate-sensitive domestic banks that lose the most if oil stays near triple digits. Keep the position paired rather than outright because Pezeshkian's ceasefire feeler can still trigger violent intraday squeezes on headlines.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Renewed Oil Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP pulled back to 59.02 on Mar 13 (-1.1%) as Pezeshkian's ceasefire feeler gave ASEAN importer FX a brief lift. The pullback is a better entry for the short-PHP bias, not a thesis reversal — Khamenei's vow that Hormuz remains blocked means crude stays above $100 and Philippine fuel-import sensitivity is intact. Brent at $100.84 keeps local fuel-inflation optics worsening if it trades through $105. Retain the 60.00-61.00 stress target; stop discipline only if a credible, accepted ceasefire appears.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Oil Import Stress Back in Play
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR is at 16,914 on Mar 13 while Indonesia 10Y has edged up to 6.72%, a combination that keeps Bank Indonesia on intervention watch rather than easing mode. Khamenei's Hormuz closure commitment and Brent at $100.84 maintain the structural oil-import pressure on IDR. Maintain the short-IDR bias, especially against renewed tanker or export-terminal disruption. Near-term stress target remains 17,200-17,500 with a tail scenario back above 18,000 if the maritime war broadens.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: The iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG proxy widened to 160bp on Mar 13 (+4bp) and the ASEAN HY composite is at 505bp (+7bp) as Khamenei's Hormuz commitment confirms credit risk stays elevated. Hold protection. The market will not re-tighten sustainably until merchant traffic is actually protected, not just debated in back-channels. If Brent breaks and holds above $105, 170-185bp remains credible; trim only if escorted flows normalise and oil re-prices below $90 for several sessions.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Keep Optionality
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM confirmed at $16.09/MMBtu on Mar 13 versus TTF at €50.27/MWh. Both legs are grinding higher as Khamenei's Hormuz closure vow and continued Qatar partial-restart status keep Asia LNG tight. The reserve release helps crude faster than LNG. Keep the JKM-over-TTF relative long for optionality rather than sizing it as the core book. If escorted LNG transits improve materially cut exposure quickly; if Gulf outages spread, JKM can catch up sharply.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's 2026-2027 refinancing window is effectively closed. Brent at $100.84 supports upstream cashflow eventually, but iTraxx Asia IG at 160bp and the ASEAN HY composite at 505bp keep unsecured funding shut. Khamenei's Hormuz closure vow extends the risk-premium horizon. Push immediately toward secured bank lines, private credit, and DFI-backed structures. Do not wait for market normalisation that may not arrive before Sapura's near-term maturities.

PTTEP should pre-fund while Thailand 10Y remains at 1.93% and THB has temporarily strengthened to 31.76 on Pezeshkian's ceasefire feeler. Brent at $100.84 materially improves upstream EBITDA, but Khamenei's closure vow means another leg higher remains credible — and that would pull Thai inflation expectations up and close the local-duration window. Execute the H2 2026 prefunding plan now and run a sanctions/compliance review on any Gulf-exposed JV structure in parallel.

Expand USD revolving-credit capacity now. At Brent $100.84, Wilmar's bunker and feedstock burden is at the top of the March stress range with USD/SGD at 1.2787. Khamenei's Hormuz closure vow and Pezeshkian's conditional ceasefire terms leave freight and insurance risk unresolved well into Q2. Use the still-functional IG bank market to extend tenor and add committed liquidity before another jump in freight or sanctions screening tightens trade-finance appetite.

Hyflux should be on an urgent restructuring track. Base probability is 22% while the stress case is 54%, Brent is $100.84, and ASEAN HY is 505bp. Khamenei's Hormuz vow pushes any near-term market normalisation off the table. That combination keeps the unsecured market effectively closed and worsens power-cost pressure on desalination assets. Engage private-credit and quasi-sovereign options now; do not wait for a cleaner market window that may not arrive while Gulf shipping stays impaired.
